summary: Completed comprehensive testing of FairFare traveler-friendly language features. All features working correctly: Traveler-friendly demand labels (Good time to ride, Normal demand, Busy — expect delays, High demand — consider waiting), Recommendation banner with actionable advice, Decision hint with situational context, Long trip modal (>150 miles), Deep link buttons, and 'Live price shown in app' message.
passed_tests: Backend: API root endpoint returns 200 OK
Backend: Traveler-friendly labels (Good time to ride, Normal demand, Busy — expect delays, High demand — consider waiting) returned
Backend: Old labels (Cheap/Moderate/Busy/Favorable/Balanced/Elevated/Peak) NOT used
Backend: Recommendation field with actionable advice present
Backend: Decision hint with situational context present
Backend: Surge likelihood values (Low/Moderate/High) working
Backend: Long trip (>150 miles) returns requires_confirmation: true with route_status: 'long_trip'
Backend: Short trip (<150 miles) does NOT require confirmation
Backend: Medium trip (~100 miles) does NOT require confirmation
Backend: Very long routes (>1000 miles) rejected with 400 error
Backend: Missing pickup coordinates returns 400
Backend: Missing destination coordinates returns 400
Backend: Availability (Good/Limited/Busy) present
Backend: No numeric prices in response
Backend: Deep links for Uber and Lyft present
Backend: ETA minutes (wait time) present
Backend: Distance and duration in response
Backend: Route status in response
Backend: Coordinate info in response
Frontend: Recommendation banner displays at top of results
Frontend: Decision hint displayed below distance/duration
Frontend: Traveler-friendly label 'Good time to ride' displayed with green color
Frontend: No 'Demand estimate' or 'Demand Estimate' labels (removed as expected)
Frontend: Wait time shows 'X min away' format
Frontend: 'Surge Likelihood: Low' displayed as separate line
Frontend: 'Live price shown in app' message displayed
Frontend: FairFare Pick card visible
Frontend: Uber and Lyft cards visible
Frontend: Long trip modal appears for routes >150 miles
Frontend: Modal shows 'Long Trip Detected' title
Frontend: Modal shows distance (190.22 miles) and duration (~253 min)
Frontend: Modal has 'Yes, Continue' button
Frontend: Modal has 'Go Back' button
Frontend: 'Yes, Continue' proceeds to results page with long trip recommendation
Frontend: Long trip recommendation: 'Recommended: Book now and confirm driver before starting.'
Frontend: Long trip decision hint: 'Long trip (190 miles) — plan for extended travel time.'
Frontend: 'Continue in Uber' button present
Frontend: 'Continue in Lyft' button present
context_for_next_testing_agent: Traveler-friendly language features verified working: 1) Demand labels (Good time to ride, Normal demand, Busy — expect delays, High demand — consider waiting) with correct colors (green/blue/orange/red), 2) Recommendation banner at top of results with actionable advice, 3) Decision hint with situational context, 4) Long trip modal for routes >150 miles with 'Yes, Continue' and 'Go Back' buttons, 5) Routes >1000 miles still rejected, 6) 'Demand estimate' label removed, 7) 'Surge Likelihood' as separate line, 8) 'Live price shown in app' message, 9) Wait time shows 'X min away' format, 10) Deep link buttons functional. Backend is entirely MOCKED - no real Uber/Lyft API calls.
mocked_api_note: Backend is entirely MOCKED - no real Uber/Lyft API calls. Decision engine uses time-based heuristics for demand/surge indicators.
